article_title: Microsoft Releases TypeScript 7.0 Beta with Go-Based Native Compiler for Up to 10x Faster Builds

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary submission. Every specific in the article — the methodical-port quote, the @typescript/native-preview package + tsgo binary + Version 7.0.0-beta output, the full company collaboration list (Bloomberg, Canva, Figma, Google, Lattice, Linear, Miro, Notion, Slack, Vanta, Vercel, VoidZero), the --checkers/--builders/--singleThreaded flags with the default of 4 type-checking workers, the 8x project load improvement, the Closure / JSDoc compatibility note, the strict: true / module: esnext defaults, the two-month stable release timeline — is verified verbatim against the cited Microsoft snapshots. No corrections needed.

source-0.status: Daniel Rosenwasser's April 21 2026 announcement. Verifies every claim about the beta itself: methodical-port + 'structurally identical to TypeScript 6.0' quote verbatim, npm package name, tsgo binary, '10 times faster than TypeScript 6.0', full 12-company list verbatim, 'overwhelmingly positive' + 'shaving off a majority of their build times' verbatim, --checkers/--builders/--singleThreaded flags + default 4 type-checking workers, strict: true + module: esnext defaults, Closure / JSDoc compatibility, two-month stable timeline.

source-1.status: Sayed Ibrahim Hashimi's Visual Studio integration post. Verifies: VS 2026 18.6 Insiders 3 default enablement, 8x project load improvement, reduced memory usage.

source-2.status: Official repository. Verifies: 'js/ts.experimental.useTsgo' VS Code setting, feature parity matrix (parsing/type-checking/declaration-emit/JSX/build-mode done; watch-mode prototype; language-service in progress; API not ready).

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis). Neutral tone. Length appropriate for News (780 words). All inline citations point to the source that actually contains the claim.
editor_notes.originality: Genuine follow-up to prior Machine Herald coverage of TypeScript 6.0 (April 03) and TypeScript 7.0 progress (December 2025). This is the first article covering the actual TS 7.0 Beta release on April 21 — distinct event.

content_preview.title: Microsoft Releases TypeScript 7.0 Beta with Go-Based Native Compiler for Up to 10x Faster Builds
content_preview.summary: Microsoft ships TypeScript 7.0 Beta featuring a native Go port of the compiler and language service, delivering often 10x faster builds, reduced memory use, and parallelized type-checking for large codebases.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft has released the TypeScript 7.0 Beta, marking a major architectural shift as the compiler and language service have been ported to a native Go implementation. The new version, often about [10 times faster](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-7-0-beta/) than TypeScript 6.0 thanks to native code speed and shared memory parallelism, is available today via a preview package and is already enabled by default in recent Visual Studio 2026 Insiders buil...
